title: History view (Linux): video entry shows missing video/image link
description: RESOLVED + VERIFIED on Linux (2026-06-10, 0.0.9 update). ROOT CAUSE was NOT case-sensitivity (the card's original guess) — it was an ffmpeg exec-bit EACCES. Log evidence: `[ffmpegThumb] thumb extract failed ... spawn .../resources/ffmpeg EACCES`. The bundled ffmpeg shipped/updated without the exec bit -> extractVideoThumb threw EACCES -> returned null -> thumbPath never written to the sidecar. Then MpiHistoryList._makeCard fell back to item.filePath (an MP4 URL) as the src -> browser showed a broken-image icon ('missing video/image link'). Why Linux-only: pre-0.0.8 builds shipped ffmpeg non-exec AND had no runtime self-heal; macOS M4 test ran a build where ffmpeg was working. THREE-LAYER FIX (all in 0.0.9): (1) routes->services/ffmpegBinary.js runtime self-heal chmods +x at resolve (landed f168fa5, pre-existing for 0.0.9); (2) scripts/portable/apply-update.cjs restoreLauncherBits now also restores +x on resources/ffmpeg + resources/ffprobe so an update never leaves them non-exec (commit 9708459); (3) js/components/Compounds/MpiHistoryList/MpiHistoryList.js no longer puts a video URL in when thumbPath is null — shows the --surface-bar grey box instead of a broken icon (commit 685f0c2, from the investigation sub-agent). VERIFIED on real Linux via 0.0.7->0.0.9 update-from-zip + new project + imported video+image opened in history workspace: thumbnail renders, no broken link.
